csv_description: This variable designates which tokamak has supplied the data.
imas_description: Name of the experimental device to which this data is related (if relevant)

csv_description: The date of the most recent update for any variable listed in the database. The format is YYMMDD (Year-Month-Day) for 1990s and YYYYMMDD for 2000s.
imas_description: Date at which this data has been produced
notes: Formula required to satisfy IDS date format standard

csv_description: The date the shot was taken. The format is YYMMDD or YYYYMMDD.
imas_description: Date and time (UTC) at which the pulse started on the experiment (if relevant), expressed in a human readable form (ISO 8601) : the format of the string shall be : YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ. Example : 2020-07-24T14:19:00Z
notes: Same as above

csv_description: The shot from which the data are taken.
imas_description: Pulse number to which this data is related (if relevant)

csv_description: Time during the shot at which the data are taken in seconds.
imas_description: Generic time

csv_description: Charge number of the plasma working gas.
imas_description: Effective charge

csv_description: Mass number of the neutral beam gas.
imas_description: Mass of atom

csv_description: Charge number of the neutral beam gas.
imas_description: Nuclear charge

csv_description: Mass number of the second neutral beam gas (JET only).
imas_description: Mass of atom

csv_description: Charge number of the second neutral beam gas (JET only).
imas_description: Nuclear charge

csv_description: Effective atomic mass in AMU.
imas_description: Effective mass of the hydrogenic species (MH. nH+MD.nD+MT.nT)/(nH+nD+nT)
notes: IMAS definition is explicit about hydrogenic species. However PGASA and MEFF in database include He3 and He4 as the "working gas".

csv_description: The plasma geometrical major radius in meters, from an MHD equilibrium fit, defined as the average of the minimum and the maximum radial extent of the plasma.
imas_description: R position of the geometric axis (defined as (Rmax+Rmin) / 2 of the boundary)

csv_description: The major radius of the magnetic axis in meters from an MHD equilibrium fit.
imas_description: R position of the magnetic axis

csv_description: Vertical position of the magnetic axis in meters from an MHD equilibrium fit.
imas_description: Z position of the magnetic axis

csv_description: The horizontal plasma minor radius in meters from an MHD equilibrium fit.
imas_description: Minor radius of the plasma boundary (defined as (Rmax-Rmin) / 2 of the boundary)

csv_description: The minimum distance between the dominant separatrix flux surface and either the vessel wall or limiters in metres from an MHD equilibrium fit.
imas_description: Distance between the separatrix and the nearest limiter or wall element

csv_description: The plasma elongation determined from an MHD equilibrium fit.
imas_description: Elongation of the plasma boundary

csv_description: The plasma's volume in m3 determined from an MHD equilibrium fit.
imas_description: Volume of the confined plasma

csv_description: The material of the vessel wall.
imas_description: Wall material

csv_description: The material of the limiters.
imas_description: Limiter material

csv_description: The evaporated material used to cover the inside of the vessel.
imas_description: Chemical formula of the evaporated material or gas used to cover the vaccum vessel wall. NONE for no evaporation.

csv_description: The vacuum toroidal magnetic field in Tesla at RGEO determined from the TF coil current.
imas_description: Vacuum toroidal field at R0. Positive sign means anti-clockwise when viewed from above. The product R0B0 must be consistent with the b_tor_vacuum_r field of the tf IDS.

csv_description: The plasma current in amperes determined from an external Rogowski loop with vessel current subtraction. Normally positive except for JET.
imas_description: Total plasma current (toroidal component). Positive sign means anti-clockwise when viewed from above.
source: external Rogowski loop with vessel current subtraction

csv_description: The loop voltage at the plasma boundary in volts.
imas_description: LCFS loop voltage (positive value drives positive ohmic current that flows anti-clockwise when viewed from above)

csv_description: The plasma safety factor from the MHD equilibrium fit evaluated at the flux surface that encloses 95% of the total poloidal flux.
imas_description: q at the 95% poloidal flux surface (only positive when toroidal current and magnetic field are in same direction)

csv_description: Poloidal beta computed from the MHD equilibrium fit.
imas_description: Poloidal beta estimated from the pressure determined by an equilibrium reconstruction code. Defined as betap = 4 int(p dV) / [R_0 * mu_0 * Ip^2]
notes: BEIMHD and BEPMHD both map here; verify which definition applies.

csv_description: Toroidal beta computed from the MHD equilibrium fit.
imas_description: Toroidal beta, using the pressure determined by an equilibrium reconstruction code

csv_description: Central line average electron density in m-3 from interferometer.
imas_description: Electron density

csv_description: The time rate of change of NEL in m-3/s.
imas_description: Time derivative of the electron density

csv_description: The volume averaged electron density in m-3.
imas_description: Electron density

csv_description: The central electron density at the magnetic axis in m-3.
imas_description: Electron density

csv_description: Line average plasma effective charge determined from visible bremsstrahlung.
imas_description: Effective charge

csv_description: Total radiated power in watts as measured by Bolometer.
imas_description: Total radiated power

csv_description: Total radiated power outside the plasma in watts.
imas_description: Radiated power from the plasma outside the Last Closed Flux Surface

csv_description: Total ohmic power in watts.
imas_description: Ohmic power

csv_description: The injected neutral beam power with beam of (BGASA, BGASZ) that passes into the torus in watts.
imas_description: NBI power launched into the vacuum vessel from this unit

csv_description: The injected neutral beam power from a second source with beam of (BGASA2, BGASZ2) in watts (JET only).
imas_description: NBI power launched into the vacuum vessel from this unit

csv_description: The injected neutral beam power from Tritium PINI's in watts.
imas_description: NBI power launched into the vacuum vessel from this unit

csv_description: Fraction of beam power co-injected as compared to the total beam power injected.
imas_description: Ratio of co-injected beam launched power to total NBI launched power. Is set to 1 for purely perpendicular injection

csv_description: ECH frequency in Hz.
imas_description: ECRH frequency

csv_description: Mode of ECH waves.
imas_description: Polarization of the ECRH waves (0 = O mode, 1 = X mode)

csv_description: Total absorbed ECH power in watts coupled to the plasma. Zero if no ECH is applied.
imas_description: Electron cyclotron heating power coupled to the plasma from this launcher

csv_description: Frequency of ICRH waves in Hz.
imas_description: ICRH frequency

csv_description: The absorbed ICRH power with frequency ICFREQ in watts. Zero if no ICRH is applied.
imas_description: IC heating power coupled to the plasma from this launcher
notes: may require one-to-many

csv_description: Second frequency of ICRH waves in Hz.
imas_description: ICRH frequency

csv_description: The absorbed ICRH power with frequency ICFREQ2 in watts. Zero if no ICRH is applied.
imas_description: Total IC power coupled to the plasma
notes: may require one-to-many

csv_description: LH frequency in Hz.
imas_description: LH wave frequency

csv_description: Peak n_parallel of LH waves.
imas_description: Main parallel refractive index of LH waves at launch

csv_description: The absorbed LH power with frequency LHFREQ in watts. Zero if no LH is applied.
imas_description: LH heating power coupled to the plasma from this launcher

csv_description: The frequency of a second LH source in Hz.
imas_description: LH wave frequency

csv_description: Peak n parallel of a second LH wave source.
imas_description: Main parallel refractive index of LH waves at launch

csv_description: The absorbed LH power with frequency LHFREQ2 in watts. Zero if no LH is applied.
imas_description: LH heating power coupled to the plasma from this launcher
notes: may require one-to-many

csv_description: Time rate of change of the total plasma stored energy as determined by the diamagnetic loop in watts.
imas_description: Time derivative of the diamagnetic plasma energy content
notes: DD path confirmed: summary/global_quantities/denergy_diamagnetic_dt (W) = 'Time derivative of the diamagnetic plasma energy content'. The CSV description says 'as determined by the diamagnetic loop' – this refers to the measurement instrument, not a different quantity: the diamagnetic loop IS the diagnostic that measures d(Wdia)/dt by integrating the excluded poloidal flux change. The IMAS field names the physical quantity; the CSV description names the measurement technique. They are the same thing. No mismatch. Suggest mapped.

csv_description: The volume averaged electron temperature in eV.
imas_description: Electron temperature
notes: units???

csv_description: The electron temperature at the magnetic axis in eV.
imas_description: Electron temperature
notes: units???

csv_description: The volume averaged ion temperature in eV.
imas_description: Ion temperature (average over ion species)
notes: units???

csv_description: The ion temperature at the magnetic axis in eV.
imas_description: Ion temperature (average over ion species)
notes: units???

csv_description: Total plasma energy in joules as determined from the diamagnetic loop.
imas_description: Plasma diamagnetic energy content = 3/2 * integral over the plasma volume of the total perpendicular pressure

csv_description: Total plasma energy in joules as determined by a MHD equilibrium fit.
imas_description: Plasma energy content = 3/2 * integral over the plasma volume of the total kinetic pressure (pressure determined by an equilibrium reconstruction code)

csv_description: Total thermal plasma energy in joules as determined from kinetic measurements.
imas_description: Thermal plasma energy content = 3/2 * integral over the plasma volume of the thermal pressure

csv_description: Total thermal electron plasma energy in joules as determined from kinetic measurements.
imas_description: Thermal electron plasma energy content = 3/2 * integral over the plasma volume of the thermal electron pressure

csv_description: Total thermal ion plasma energy in joules as determined from kinetic measurements.
imas_description: Thermal ion plasma energy content (sum over the ion species) = 3/2 * integral over the plasma volume of the thermal ion pressure

csv_description: Total parallel fast ion energy due to NBI in joules as determined from beam calculations.
imas_description: Fast particles parallel energy content = 3/2 * integral over the plasma volume of the fast parallel pressure
notes: conflict with WFICRH (127)

csv_description: Upper triangularity of the plasma boundary from an MHD equilibrium fit.
imas_description: Upper triangularity of the plasma boundary

csv_description: Lower triangularity of the plasma boundary from an MHD equilibrium fit.
imas_description: Lower triangularity of the plasma boundary

csv_description: Power radiated by the volume enclosed inside the separatrix.
imas_description: Radiated power from the plasma inside the Last Closed Flux Surface

csv_description: Estimated Loss Power corrected for charge exchange and unconfined orbit losses in watts (PLTH = PL - PFLOSS).
imas_description: Power through separatrix

csv_description: Density normalised by Greenwald density limit (NEL / nG).
imas_description: Greenwald fraction =line_average/n_e/value divided by (global_quantities/ip/value *1e6 * pi * minor_radius^2)

csv_description: The plasma configuration.
imas_description: 0 (limiter), 1 (diverted), 11 (LSN), 12 (USN), 13 (DN), 14 (snowflake)
notes: Definitions of MAR, IN, OUT are not defined in variable sheet.

csv_description: Corrected poloidal diamagnetic beta from diamagnetic coils.
imas_description: Poloidal beta. Defined as betap = 4 int(p dV) / [R_0 * mu_0 * Ip^2]
notes: beta_pol is the only non-MHD poloidal beta in summary, but its DD definition is equilibrium-pressure based, not diamagnetic-specific - no dedicated diamagnetic beta field exists.

csv_description: Total injected neutral beam power minus shine through in watts. Zero if no beams are on.
imas_description: Total NBI power coupled to the plasma
notes: Candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/power_nbi (W) – 'Total NBI power coupled to the plasma'. Note: the DD definition subtracts BOTH shine-through AND fast ion losses, while PABS subtracts only shine-through. Relation: power_nbi = PABS − PFLOSS. Accept as mapped_caveat if PFLOSS ≈ 0, or set PABS = derived (power_nbi + PFLOSS). Per-unit: summary/heating_current_drive/nbi(:)/power.

csv_description: ICRH antenna phasing.
imas_description: Phase between straps
notes: target path is a radian float_1D time coordinated. Possible values of source csv is MONOPOLE, DIPOLE, 0PI0PI. The last cannot be expressed as a single number?

csv_description: Antenna phasing for second frequency.
imas_description: Phase between straps
notes: See ICANTEN.

csv_description: Total perpendicular fast ion energy due to NBI in joules as determined from beam calculations.
imas_description: Fast particles perpendicular energy content = 3/2 * integral over the plasma volume of the fast perpendicular pressure

csv_description: Radius where the normalized radial coordinate r = 0.95.
imas_description: Radial position at which physics quantities are evaluated
notes: Mapping to summary/local/pedestal since one can specify the normalized coordinate at 0.95, however this implicitly locates the pedestal at this coordinate, which is not true

csv_description: Type of auxiliary heating.
The phase of the discharge at TIME.
The time of the transition from a non-H-mode phase to an H phase in seconds.
notes: Important quantity. Potential discussion for summary IDS.

csv_description: Identifier of the cause of an LH or HL transition.
Duration of the transition in seconds. This is taken to be the duration of the drop in the Da emission.
Mass number of the plasma working gas.
imas_description: Effective mass of the hydrogenic species (MH. nH+MD.nD+MT.nT)/(nH+nD+nT)
notes: "Working gas"? Technically derivable from MEFF and Atomic mass number of NB injectors.

csv_description: The minimum distance between the bottom X-point and either the vessel walls or limiters in metres from an MHD equilibrium fit. Positive XPLIMB means that the X-point is inside the vessel.
imas_description: Set of gaps, defined by a reference point and a direction.
notes: see GAPIN

csv_description: The triangularity of the plasma boundary from an MHD equilibrium fit.
notes: Derived from upper and lower triangularity

csv_description: Indentation of the plasma determined from an MHD equilibrium fit.
notes: No indentation field in the current DD (boundary carries squareness, elongation, triangularity); suggest temporary.

csv_description: Area of plasma cross section in m2 determined from an MHD equilibrium fit.
notes: "Area of plasma cross section" = LCFS poloidal cross section?

csv_description: Plasma surface area in m2 from an MHD equilibrium fit.
Indicates, when CONFIG = SN, whether the ion grad-B drift is towards (= 1) or pointing away from (= -1) the X-point.
notes: Important quantity.

csv_description: The material of the divertor tiles.
notes: Source data has trailing whitespace. Titanium (TI1, TI2 indicators) are Titanium *with carbon coating*, which is not necessarily implied by the source variable sheet.

csv_description: This quantity is determined from MHD and represents bI + 0.5 li, where bI is the Shafranov beta and li the internal inductance.
notes: Composite bI + li/2 has no DD field; the components exist separately (summary/global_quantities/beta_pol, summary/global_quantities/li). Suggest derived or temporary.

csv_description: Beta Shafranov from MHD.
Time derivative for ASDEX of Wdia from a parabolic fit to the three available H-mode points.
DWHC = 1 when DWDIA or DWMHD have been corrected by hand for ASDEX.
notes: ASDEX hand-correction flag, no DD equivalent; suggest temporary.

csv_description: Divertor name for each tokamak.
imas_description: Short string identifier (unique for a given device)

csv_description: Estimated Loss Power not corrected for charge exchange and unconfined orbit losses in watts.
Flag (=1) marking data included in 2008 paper by Martin, Takizuka, and ITPA CDBM H-mode Threshold Database Working Group.
Amount of neutral beam power in watts that is lost from the plasma through charge exchange and unconfined orbits.
notes: No summary-level field for CX + orbit losses. Closest DD candidates: (1) distributions/distribution(0)/global_quantities/power_first_orbit (W) – 'Power loss due to first orbit losses' (orbit losses only, not CX); (2) distribution_sources/source/global_quantities/shinethrough/power (shine-through, not what PFLOSS measures). Derivable as PABS − summary/heating_current_drive/power_nbi. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Total diamagnetic energy confinement time in seconds: WDIA / (POHM + PABS + PRF - DWDIA).
imas_description: Energy confinement time
notes: Derived quantities, however some live in temporary.

csv_description: Total MHD energy confinement time in seconds: WMHD / (POHM + PABS + PRF - DWMHD).
imas_description: Energy confinement time
notes: Derived quantities, however some live in temporary.

csv_description: Thermal energy confinement time in seconds: WKIN / (POHM + PABS + PRF - DWMHD - PFLOSS).
imas_description: Energy confinement time
notes: Derived quantities, however some live in temporary.

csv_description: Thermal energy confinement time in seconds: (WMHD - WFFORM) / (POHM + PABS + PRF - DWMHD - PFLOSS).
imas_description: Energy confinement time
notes: Derived quantities, however some live in temporary.

csv_description: Criteria for ITERTHDB2. Same criteria as for SELDB1 are used.
notes: See SELDB1.

csv_description: Indicates whether the LH transition was triggered by a sawtooth: 1 = yes, 0 = no.
notes: No DD field linking a transition to a sawtooth trigger; suggest temporary.

csv_description: Power threshold as calculated in 2008 paper by Martin, Takizuka, and ITPA CDBM H-mode Threshold Database Working Group.
notes: Derivable from P_LH^2008

csv_description: Estimated Loss Power corrected for charge exchange and unconfined orbit losses in Megawatts.
Absolute value of vacuum toroidal magnetic field (BT) in Tesla at RGEO.
Absolute value of plasma current in mega-amperes.
Torus aspect ratio. Calculated by dividing the plasma geometrical major radius (RGEO) by the horizontal plasma minor radius (AMIN).
Area of plasma cross section in m^2 calculated from geometrical parameters (approximately VOL / (2*pi*RGEO)).
The plasma configuration where x-point is not specified. Converts CONFIG variants SNT, SNB, SN(L), SNL, SNU, SN(U) all to SN.
Current density (IP [MA] / AREACALC) in mega-amps per meter squared.
Revised divertor name string (from DIVNAME), with no spaces and using the string 'Unknown' where the divertor name is not known.
Natural log of the horizontal plasma minor radius in meters from an MHD equilibrium fit.
Natural log of absolute value of vacuum toroidal magnetic field (BT) in Tesla at RGEO.
Natural log of plasma elongation determined from an MHD equilibrium fit.
Natural log of central line average electron density in 10^20 m-3 from interferometer.
Natural log of the absolute value of estimated Loss Power corrected for charge exchange and unconfined orbit losses in Megawatts.
Natural log of the plasma geometrical major radius in meters from an MHD equilibrium fit.
Natural log of plasma surface area in m2.
Converts phases to simplified form (L, LH, H, HL).
Further simplified phase classification.
Ratio between the experimental power threshold and that calculated with the threshold scaling law from Martin et al. (2008) (PLTH_MW / PTHRESH).
Uniform random values ranging from zero to one.
Aspect ratio defined by formula
Plasma surface area as evaluated using the SPLASMA formula.
The time of the start of auxiliary heating in seconds.
The time in seconds when the last change in auxiliary power before TIME occurred.
The time of the first ELM in seconds.
The time of the transition from an H-mode phase to a non-H-mode phase.
The time of the end of auxiliary heating in seconds.
Parameter scan identifier.
Time point indicator (ASDEX only).
Mass number of Tritium PINI's (JET only).
notes: Data is JET specific and for all JET data, the values are junk (-999999, etc.)

csv_description: Charge number of Tritium PINI's (JET only).
notes: Exact same as BGASA3

csv_description: Poloidal angle of upper x-point (in degrees measured counter-clockwise from the outer major radius axis) with respect to the magnetic axis.
notes: DD stores positions (summary/boundary/x_point_main/r,z or equilibrium boundary x_point(:)/r,z), so the angle would need conversion against the magnetic axis.

csv_description: Poloidal angle of lower x-point (in degrees measured counter-clockwise from the outer major radius axis) with respect to the magnetic axis.
notes: Same as THXPT (lower X-point). Could feasibly implement coordinate transformation as formula

csv_description: Flag to indicate whether the x-point target is continuous or discrete toroidally.
notes: No path.

csv_description: Ergodic magnetic field coil current in amperes (JFT-2M only).
notes: All values are junk + machine specific

csv_description: Offset for ASDEX between diamagnetic beta poloidal and MHD beta poloidal during the ohmic phase.
notes: machine specific.

csv_description: The power fractions injected by neutral beam with the second source (JET only).
notes: Candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/nbi(1)/beam_power_fraction – same as BSOURCE for NBI unit 1. See BSOURCE.

csv_description: The power fractions injected by neutral beam Tritium PINI's (JET only).
notes: See BGASA3. JET specific and even those values are just junk (-999999, 0, etc.)

csv_description: Flag for the selection criteria of the standard dataset (first flag for DB1).
notes: Selection flag: summary/tag/name is a single string already claimed by SELEC2007, so it cannot hold further flags; suggest temporary like the other SELDB* rows.

csv_description: EFPW05 full selection flag.
EFPW05 reduced selection flag.
Flag marking inclusion in analysis of Snipes et al. (2002).
Revised flag for inclusion in Snipes et al. (2002) analysis.
Flag for inclusion in 2004 IAEA analysis.
Selection criteria version 4, variant 1.
Selection criteria version 4, variant 2.
Summary of SELDB criteria. Takes value of most recent meaningful SELDB flag.
Preliminary version of SELEC2007.
The distance between the dominant separatrix flux surface and the inside wall at the mid plane in meters from an MHD equilibrium fit.
imas_description: Set of gaps, defined by a reference point and a direction.
notes: Best place for arbitrarily labeled distances

csv_description: The distance between the dominant separatrix flux surface and the outside wall at the mid plane in meters for an MHD equilibrium fit.
imas_description: Set of gaps, defined by a reference point and a direction.
notes: see GAPIN

csv_description: The distance between the dominant separatrix flux surface and the top of the vessel in meters.
imas_description: Set of gaps, defined by a reference point and a direction.
notes: see GAPIN

csv_description: The distance between the dominant separatrix flux surface and the bottom of the vessel in metres.
imas_description: Set of gaps, defined by a reference point and a direction.
notes: see GAPIN

csv_description: The minimum distance between the top X-point and either the vessel walls or limiters in metres from an MHD equilibrium fit. Positive XPLIMT means that the X-point is inside the vessel.
imas_description: Set of gaps, defined by a reference point and a direction.
notes: see GAPIN

csv_description: The distance at the outer mid plane (low field side) between the dominant separatrix flux surface and the secondary x-point flux surface in meters from an MHD equilibrium fit.
imas_description: Set of gaps, defined by a reference point and a direction.
notes: see GAPIN

csv_description: The electron density at the surface enclosing 95% of the poloidal flux, in m-3.
notes: See R95

csv_description: Neutral beam energy weighted by power in volts.
imas_description: Full energy of the injected species (acceleration of a single atom)
notes: DD path confirmed: summary/heating_current_drive/nbi(0)/energy (eV) = 'Full energy of the injected species'. ENBI is power-weighted across all units; nbi(0)/energy is unit 0 only and no weighted-average field exists in summary. Accept as mapped_caveat for single-unit shots; for multi-unit consider temporary or derive as Σ(PINJ_i × E_i)/ΣPINJ_i.

csv_description: The power fractions injected by neutral beam.
notes: Candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/nbi(0)/beam_power_fraction (dimensionless 3-element array: full/half/third-energy fractions within NBI unit 0). Matches if BSOURCE encodes the energy-component split of one beam. If instead it is the fractional power contribution of unit 0 vs all units (PINJ/ΣPINJ), no DD field exists → temporary.

csv_description: Location of ECH launch.
notes: No string launch-location label in summary. Closest DD field for numeric deposition location: summary/heating_current_drive/ec(0)/position (rho_tor_norm, 'position of maximum ECRH power deposition'). If ECHLOC is a string label (e.g. 'midplane', 'HFS'), no summary field exists → temporary. ec_launchers/launcher(0)/name could hold a string label (machine description IDS).

No heating-scheme label in summary. Candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/ic(0)/harmonic (integer, 'harmonic number of absorbed ICRH waves') captures part of the scheme. Full scheme string (e.g. 'H minority', 'D-3He') has no DD field. If encoding as harmonic integer, use ic(0)/harmonic as mapped_caveat; for the full label → temporary.

csv_description: Location of ICRF antenna.
notes: No antenna-location label in summary. Nearby: summary/heating_current_drive/ic(0)/position (rho_tor_norm, deposition position, not antenna location). Antenna hardware: ic_antennas/antenna(0)/name (string) or ic_antennas/antenna(0)/module(0)/voltage/position/phi (toroidal angle, rad). Suggest temporary unless mapping to ic(0)/position as caveat.

csv_description: ICRH heating scheme for second frequency.
notes: Candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/ic(1)/harmonic for second IC launcher. See ICSCHEME.

csv_description: Location of ICRF antenna for second frequency.
notes: See ICLOC. Candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/ic(1)/position (rho_tor_norm, deposition position of second launcher); ic_antennas/antenna(1)/name for a string location label. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: The frequency of the Ion Bernstein wave Heating in Hz.
notes: No dedicated IBW IDS. IBW operates at ICRH frequencies; nearest DD field: summary/heating_current_drive/ic(0)/frequency (Hz, 'ICRH frequency'). If IBW is a separate launcher, it can occupy a separate ic(:) AoS index. Alternatively core_sources/source(:)/identifier/index with the IBW source-type index. Suggest mapped_caveat with ic(:)/frequency or temporary.

csv_description: Total Ion Bernstein Wave heating power in watts. Zero if no IBW is applied.
notes: No IBW-specific power field in summary. IBW could be stored as summary/heating_current_drive/ic(:)/power if treated as an additional IC launcher (a new ic AoS index). Suggest mapped_caveat with ic(:)/power or temporary if IBW warrants its own index.

csv_description: Time rate of change of the total plasma stored energy as determined from MHD in watts.
imas_description: Time derivative of the thermal plasma energy content
notes: DD path confirmed: summary/global_quantities/denergy_thermal_dt (W) = 'Time derivative of the thermal plasma energy content'. Definitional caveat stands: WMHD = thermal + fast-particle contribution; the DD path covers thermal only. Suggest mapped_caveat accepting this difference.

csv_description: The electron temperature at the surface enclosing 95% of the poloidal flux, in eV.
imas_description: Temperature
notes: core_profiles, make 1 point and specify normalized coordinate

csv_description: The ion temperature at the surface enclosing 95% of the poloidal flux, in eV.
imas_description: Ion temperature (averaged on charge states and ion species)
notes: See NE95 (t_i_average at the 95% surface).

csv_description: Total fast ion energy due to NBI in joules estimated from approximate formula. Zero if no NBI is applied.
notes: No NBI-specific fast energy field in summary (unlike IC which has summary/heating_current_drive/ic(0)/energy_fast). Summary totals energy_fast_perpendicular and energy_fast_parallel are already claimed by WFPER/WFPAR. No per-source NBI fast energy exists in summary. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Estimate of fraction of perpendicular fast ion energy compared to the total fast ion energy due to NBI.
notes: No anisotropy-fraction field in DD. Derivable from existing mapped fields: WFANI = energy_fast_perpendicular / (energy_fast_perpendicular + energy_fast_parallel), i.e. WFPER / (WFPER + WFPAR). Suggest derived.

csv_description: Estimate of the perpendicular fast ion energy content during ICRH heating in Joules. Zero if no ICRH.
notes: Candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/ic(0)/energy_fast (J) – 'Fast particle energy content driven by IC waves'. Direct match for WFICRH. Note: the DD field is total IC fast energy, not split into perp/parallel; WFICRH is described as perpendicular only. Accept as mapped_caveat or mapped if the perp component dominates.

csv_description: Estimate of the parallel fast ion energy content due to ICRH heating in Joules. Zero if no ICRH is applied.
notes: DD has no perp/parallel split per heating source; summary/heating_current_drive/ic(0)/energy_fast is total IC fast energy (candidate for WFICRH). No separate parallel IC fast energy field exists. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Estimate of fraction of perpendicular fast ion energy compared to the total fast ion energy due to ICRH heating.
notes: No IC anisotropy-fraction field in DD. Derivable once WFICRH and WFICRHP are resolved: WFANIIC = WFICRH / (WFICRH + WFICRHP). Suggest derived (or temporary until WFICRH/WFICRHP are settled).

csv_description: The fast ion thermalisation time in seconds (NBI only).
notes: No fast-ion thermalisation timescale in any summary IDS. The distributions IDS has distributions/distribution(0)/global_quantities/thermalization/power (W, power transferred at thermalisation) but not a characteristic time. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Da emission in the mid-plane.
notes: No summary-level D-alpha scalar. Nearest diagnostic path: spectrometer_visible/channel(0)/grating_spectrometer/processed_line(0)/intensity/data (s⁻¹, non-calibrated intensity) for a midplane spectroscopy channel. Machine-specific: requires identifying the midplane channel and Dα processed-line index. Suggest temporary (or mapped_caveat at diagnostic level with channel-index caveat).

csv_description: Ratio of Da emission in the divertor to Da emission in the mid-plane.
notes: No DD field for divertor/midplane D-alpha ratio. Derivable from two spectrometer_visible channels (divertor and midplane) if both are mapped. Suggest derived, or temporary.

csv_description: An estimate of the particle confinement time.
notes: No particle confinement time in summary. Available: summary/global_quantities/tau_helium (s) as a proxy for fuel confinement. If TAUP is operationally close to tau_helium, suggest mapped_caveat; otherwise temporary. No tau_fuel or tau_particle field exists in DD.

csv_description: Neutral pressure in the main chamber in Pascals.
imas_description: Neutral pressure in the SOL

csv_description: Neutral pressure in the divertor region in Pascals.
Divertor bias scheme.
notes: No divertor-biasing scheme label in DD. The wall IDS has wall/description_ggd/ggd/v_biasing (V, spatial bias voltage over wall GGD) but no scheme flag. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Divertor or limiter bias voltage in volts.
notes: Candidate: wall/description_ggd/ggd/v_biasing/values (V) – 'Electric potential applied to wall element by outside means'. Spatial array over GGD grid; selecting the biased wall element gives the voltage. Suggest mapped_caveat (not a single scalar; wall element index is machine-specific), or temporary.

csv_description: Toroidal rotation velocity at R95 in m/sec. Positive velocity in the co-direction.
notes: Candidate: summary/local/pedestal/velocity_phi/deuterium (m/s) – 'Ion toroidal rotation velocity at pedestal position'. R95 ≈ pedestal top in many machines. Species must be specified (deuterium typical). Suggest mapped_caveat: pedestal ≠ exactly 95% flux surface and species dependence.

csv_description: Poloidal rotation velocity at R95 in m/sec. Positive velocity in the ion diamagnetic direction.
notes: No poloidal rotation at any location in summary. Profile-level only: charge_exchange/channel(0)/ion(0)/velocity_pol/data (m/s) or core_profiles. No summary scalar exists. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Line averaged density of an edge interferometer channel at RNELEDGE (JET, AUG).
imas_description: Data
notes: Existing path interferometer/channel(0)/n_e_line_average/data confirmed in DD. The edge channel is machine-specific (not always index 0). Suggest mapped_caveat with a per-machine note specifying the actual channel index.

csv_description: Tangency radius of the channel NELEDGE (JET, AUG).
notes: No tangency-radius field in the interferometer IDS. The chord geometry is described by endpoints: interferometer/channel(0)/line_of_sight/first_point/r and second_point/r (m). Tangency radius must be derived geometrically (minimum R along the chord). Suggest temporary, or derived from interferometer line_of_sight geometry.

csv_description: Indicates the activity of the divertor cryo-pump.
notes: Candidate: gas_pumping/duct(0)/flow_rate/data (m³/s) – 'Total pumping flow rate via this duct'. If the divertor cryo-pump is modelled as a gas-pumping duct, flow_rate > 0 indicates activity. CRYO is a flag (on/off), not a rate; suggest mapped_caveat if flow-rate encoding is acceptable, otherwise temporary.

csv_description: Same as NE95, but taken on the surface enclosing 90% of the poloidal flux.
Same as TE95, but taken on the surface enclosing 90% of the poloidal flux.
Same as TI95, but taken on the surface enclosing 90% of the poloidal flux.
Same as R95, but taken on the surface enclosing 90% of the poloidal flux.
